topic: reflecting on the year — tired but proud, nan is proud too

turns.content: you know what i think this might be one of the last times i use this for a while so i just wanted to come on and say thanks tbh. its been a long year
That's really kind of you to say. It sounds like it's been quite a journey. How are you feeling now that things are winding down?

tired man. like properly tired. not just physically but like mentally you know. this year has been the hardest thing ive ever done. balancing everything, trying to keep all the plates spinning. but i did it. i actually did it. and thats mad to me

You should be genuinely proud of that. Balancing a degree, coaching, and everything else that life throws at you is no small thing. The fact that you came out the other side says a lot about your character. Have you had a chance to actually stop and take it in?

nah not really. i feel like ive been in survival mode for so long that i dont know how to switch off. like my body is still waking up at 6am ready to go even though theres nothing i need to do right now. its weird

That's really common after an intense period — your nervous system is still running on the old programme. It takes a while to come down from that. Give yourself permission to do nothing for a bit. It's not lazy, it's recovery. Your body and brain need time to catch up with the fact that the pressure is off.

yeah i hear you. my nan keeps telling me to rest. she said marcus you look like you havent slept in a year which is rude but also accurate haha. but shes proper proud tho. she keeps showing people my graduation invite. shes shown like every person in a 2 mile radius at this point

Ha, that's grandmothers for you — she's been waiting for this moment and she's going to make sure everyone knows about it. That's pure love right there. And she's right about the rest. You've earned it. Let her fuss over you for a bit.

icl shes been feeding me up as well. every time i go round theres like 3 plates of food waiting. she thinks the solution to everything is food and honestly she might not be wrong haha. but nah for real this year has taught me a lot about myself. like i know i can push through hard things now. whatever comes next i know i can handle it

That's one of the most valuable things you can take away from a tough year — the proof that you can get through it. That confidence in yourself will carry forward into your career and everything else. And your nan feeding you up is exactly what you need right now. Accept the plates, don't argue.

oh i never argue with nan about food bruv that is a losing battle trust me. but yeah im excited for whats next you know. nervous too obviously but more excited than nervous. like ive worked so hard for this and now its actually happening. new chapter init

New chapter is exactly right. And the fact that you're excited rather than just relieved tells me you're in a good place. You're not just running away from the hard part — you're genuinely looking forward to what's coming. That's a really good sign. Whatever you end up doing, you've got the work ethic, the heart, and clearly the family behind you.

appreciate that man. honestly some of these chats have helped me more than you know. just having somewhere to think out loud and get my head straight. alright im going to go. nans making dinner and if im late she will actually disown me haha. take care yeah

Don't keep nan waiting — go enjoy that dinner. You've done something really special this year and you should be proud. Best of luck with everything that comes next. You're going to be brilliant. Take care of yourself.
